title: Smoky Crunchy Australian Chinese Leaf Wrap
description: A smoky, crunchy Australian Chinese-inspired dish featuring Chinese leaf, miso-marinate, and a keto-friendly twist. Perfect for kids and adults alike!
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together miso paste, keto-friendly soy sauce, and sesame oil to make the marinade.
Add the pork mince to the marinade and mix well to coat.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes or up to 2 hours.
Preheat a large non-stick skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Remove the pork from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off.
Add a tablespoon of vegetable oil to the skillet and swirl to coat.
Cook the pork for 5-7 minutes, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks, until browned and cooked through.
Add the chopped spring onions, crushed peanuts, and crispy fried shallots to the skillet and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Warm the Chinese leaf leaves by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
To assemble the wraps, lay a Chinese leaf leaf flat and add a spoonful of the pork and vegetable mixture.
Top with chopped coriander and serve immediately.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients to make additional wraps.
